title: Ether's Biggest Corporate Holders Back New Ethereum Research Hub
summary: Ethlabs was formed with support from SharpLink, Bitmine and Consensys CEO Joe Lubin as the network's development is expanding beyond the Ethereum Foundation.
body:
A group of Ethereum's largest corporate holders has launched Ethlabs, a new research hub focused on advancing the Ethereum network. The initiative is backed by SharpLink, Bitmine, and Consensys CEO Joe Lubin, signaling a growing interest in decentralized development beyond the Ethereum Foundation.

Ethlabs aims to foster innovation and research in Ethereum's ecosystem, potentially leading to new protocols, tools, and applications. The hub will operate independently, allowing for diverse perspectives and approaches to Ethereum's development. This move comes as Ethereum continues to evolve, with ongoing upgrades and increasing adoption.

For everyday users, Ethlabs could mean faster and more innovative developments in Ethereum's infrastructure. The research hub might lead to improved scalability, security, and usability, benefiting developers and end-users alike. As Ethereum remains a cornerstone of the crypto ecosystem, these advancements could enhance the overall user experience.

Watch for announcements from Ethlabs regarding new research projects, partnerships, and potential collaborations with other Ethereum stakeholders. This could shape the future of Ethereum's development and its role in the broader blockchain landscape.

Ethlabs aims to expand Ethereum's development beyond the Ethereum Foundation, fostering innovation and new protocols. This could lead to faster, more scalable solutions.

Keep an eye on Ethlabs for new research projects and partnerships. This could shape Ethereum's future. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-22-major-ether-holders-launch-ethereum-research-hub-ethlabs
